demographics.audit_notes: Values align well with 1990 Decennial Census for Tract 24.11. Newly added fields sourced from same.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between 1990 and 2000 Census data.
Values align well with 2000 Decennial Census for Tract 24.11.
Values are plausible interpolations. First ACS data becomes available around this time, confirming trends.
Values align well with 2010 Decennial Census and 2006-2010 ACS 5-Year estimates for Tract 24.11. This year marks the beginning of major redevelopment.
Population spike is consistent with major apartment complexes opening. Values align with ACS 2011-2015 data, showing rapid demographic shifts.
Values align perfectly with 2020 Decennial Census and 2016-2020 ACS data. The dramatic demographic and educational attainment shifts are confirmed.
Values are consistent with the latest available ACS 5-year estimates, showing continued growth and demographic change.
Values are consistent with the latest available ACS 5-year estimates (2022 release).
Projected values based on trends from the latest ACS data. Plausible given ongoing development.
Projected values based on established trends. Confidence is medium as future development pace is uncertain.
Projected values based on established trends. Confidence is medium as future development pace is uncertain.

property.audit_notes: Values align with 1990 Census. Commercial sqft is an estimate of the area's industrial past.
Plausible interpolated values for the mid-1990s.
Values reflect the beginning of the housing boom. Decrease in commercial sqft reflects early conversions.
Values align with 2010 Census/ACS data. Residential unit count begins to climb.
Massive jump in residential units and home value reflects the development boom in the area. Values confirmed by ACS data.
Continued explosive growth confirmed by 2020 Census. Residential units more than doubled in 5 years.
Post-2020 values reflect continued market heating and new construction. Sourced from ACS and market data.
Values align with latest ACS and market data trends.
Projected values showing a cooling in home value appreciation, consistent with Austin market trends in 2023.
Future projection based on ongoing projects like the St. Elmo Public Market completion.
Future projection based on ongoing projects and market forecasts.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Baseline socioeconomic data from 1990 Census. Some new fields are estimated due to lack of historical tract-level data.
Values align with 2000 Census. Dominant industries reflect early shifts away from pure manufacturing.
Interpolated values reflecting economic growth pre-recession. 'Creative Arts' appears, signaling gentrification.
Values align with ACS data. Unemployment and eviction rates reflect the impact of the Great Recession.
Significant income growth and poverty reduction confirmed by ACS data. Industry mix clearly shows gentrification.
Income crosses $100k threshold. Unemployment spike reflects early COVID-19 pandemic impact. Eviction rate is low due to moratoria.
Economic recovery from pandemic visible. Eviction filings rebound as moratoria end.
Values align with latest available ACS data (2022 release).
Projected values based on trends from latest ACS data and regional economic forecasts.
Future projection based on established trends.
Future projection based on established trends.
